meta.title: Rheumatology Practice Software | RA/Lupus RTM & Biologic Management | clinIQ
meta.description: Complete guide to rheumatology practice operations. $9,750/month in rheumatic disease RTM. Biologic authorization and injection scheduling. Disease activity tracking. Infusion coordination.

hero.subhead: Your RA, lupus, and PsA patients already report symptoms between visits — that's billable RTM. Your biologics require authorization tracking. Your infusions need scheduling. clinIQ captures RTM revenue, manages biologic authorizations, and coordinates infusion therapy.

sections.content:
Rheumatology practices manage complex chronic diseases that require ongoing monitoring and expensive therapies.

**The monitoring challenge:**

Rheumatoid arthritis patients fluctuate between visits. Lupus flares unpredictably. Psoriatic arthritis patients need joint and skin monitoring. Without between-visit data, treatment decisions are based on snapshots.

**The biologic management burden:**

Most rheumatology patients are on biologics or targeted synthetics — Humira, Enbrel, Remicade, Orencia, Actemra, Xeljanz, Rinvoq, and many others. Each requires:
- Prior authorization
- Step therapy documentation
- Lab monitoring
- Injection/infusion scheduling
- Annual renewals

Without systems, authorization management consumes staff time.

**The RTM opportunity:**

75 rheumatic disease patients × $130/month = $9,750/month = $117,000/year.

This revenue captures work that should already be happening — monitoring disease activity and adjusting treatment based on patient-reported data.

RTM for rheumatic diseases captures between-visit symptom tracking that informs treatment decisions.

**What rheumatology patients track:**

Rheumatoid arthritis:
- Morning stiffness duration
- Joint pain and swelling (specific joints)
- Fatigue
- Functional limitations
- Medication compliance

Systemic lupus erythematosus:
- Fatigue and malaise
- Joint symptoms
- Skin manifestations
- Fevers
- New symptoms (renal, neurologic, cardiopulmonary)

Psoriatic arthritis:
- Joint symptoms
- Skin involvement (psoriasis activity)
- Enthesitis symptoms
- Nail changes
- Medication compliance

Ankylosing spondylitis:
- Back pain and stiffness
- Morning stiffness duration
- Peripheral joint involvement
- Functional status
- Sleep quality

**The CPT codes:**

98975: RTM setup — one-time. ~$20-25.
98977: RTM device supply. ~$50-60/month.
98980: RTM treatment management — first 20 minutes. ~$50-70/month.
98981: Additional 20-minute increments.

Typical: $120-150/patient/month.

**What clinIQ does for rheumatic RTM:**

Disease-specific templates: RA patients get joint counts. Lupus patients get organ system reviews. PsA patients get skin and joint assessment.

Flare detection: Symptom worsening triggers alerts. Morning stiffness increasing? More joints involved? Early flare signs surface.

Disease activity scoring: RAPID3, DAS28-CRP, CDAI — simplified versions administered through the app.

Medication compliance: Biologic injection compliance tracked. Missed doses flagged.

The majority of rheumatology patients are on biologics or targeted synthetics. Managing authorizations is a major operational burden.

**The biologic landscape:**

TNF inhibitors: Humira, Enbrel, Cimzia, Simponi, Remicade (and biosimilars)
IL-6 inhibitors: Actemra, Kevzara
T-cell costimulation inhibitor: Orencia
B-cell depletion: Rituxan
JAK inhibitors: Xeljanz, Rinvoq, Olumiant
IL-17 inhibitors: Cosentyx, Taltz
IL-12/23 inhibitors: Stelara

Each has different authorization requirements, monitoring needs, and renewal timelines.

**The authorization workflow:**

Initial authorization:
- Documented diagnosis with objective evidence
- Failed conventional therapy (MTX, etc.)
- Labs (TB, hepatitis, CBC, CMP)
- Disease severity documentation

Ongoing authorization:
- Annual or semi-annual renewals
- Response documentation
- Labs current
- No contraindications

**What clinIQ does for biologic authorization:**

Auth status dashboard: Every patient on biologics visible with current drug, auth status, expiration date.

Expiration alerts: 'Humira auth expires in 21 days. Patient due for injection next week.'

Step therapy documentation: MTX trial, DMARD combinations — documented with dates and outcomes.

Lab tracking: TB, hepatitis B/C, CBC, CMP, lipids (for JAK inhibitors) — status per patient.

**Switching biologics:**

Patients who fail one biologic often need another. Documentation requirements:
- Reason for switch (lack of efficacy, side effects, antibody development)
- Prior biologic duration
- Washout period (if required)

clinIQ tracks biologic history to support authorization for subsequent agents.

Many rheumatology biologics are administered as infusions:

- Remicade (infliximab): Every 4-8 weeks
- Orencia IV (abatacept): Every 4 weeks
- Actemra IV (tocilizumab): Every 4 weeks
- Rituxan (rituximab): Every 6 months typically

**The infusion coordination challenge:**

Pre-infusion requirements:
- Authorization current
- Labs current (TB screening, CBC, etc.)
- No active infection
- Pre-medications ordered

Scheduling:
- Chair availability
- Infusion duration (varies by drug)
- Nurse availability

**What clinIQ does for infusion coordination:**

Infusion pipeline: Every infusion patient visible with last infusion, next due, auth status, labs status.

Pre-infusion checklist: Auth current, labs current, pre-meds ordered, no contraindications — status visible before scheduling.

Chair scheduling: Chair availability matched to infusion duration.

Infusion day readiness: Day-of confirmation that patient is cleared for infusion.

**The revenue protection:**

Missed infusions from expired auths or missing labs = lost revenue and undertreated patients.

One missed Remicade infusion = $5,000-8,000 lost revenue + potential disease flare.

clinIQ prevents these gaps.

Rheumatology uses standardized disease activity measures:

**RA measures:**
- DAS28-CRP/ESR: Disease Activity Score
- CDAI: Clinical Disease Activity Index
- RAPID3: Routine Assessment of Patient Index Data 3
- ACR response criteria

**Lupus measures:**
- SLEDAI: Systemic Lupus Erythematosus Disease Activity Index
- BILAG: British Isles Lupus Assessment Group
- PGA: Physician Global Assessment

**PsA measures:**
- DAPSA: Disease Activity in Psoriatic Arthritis
- MDA: Minimal Disease Activity

**The tracking challenge:**

These measures require data collection at each visit. Joint counts, patient-reported outcomes, lab values. Calculating scores takes time. Trending scores over time requires organized data.

**What clinIQ does:**

Patient-reported components: RAPID3, patient global, functional assessment — collected via app or at check-in.

Provider components: Joint counts, physician global — entered during visit.

Score calculation: DAS28, CDAI, etc. calculated automatically.

Trend visualization: Disease activity over time. Response to treatment changes visible.

**The clinical value:**

Treat-to-target requires knowing where you are. Objective disease activity scores — tracked over time — drive treatment decisions and support authorization.

Rheumatology visits vary significantly:

- New patient: 45-60 minutes (complex history, joint exam, assessment)
- Established follow-up: 20-30 minutes
- Flare visit: 30-45 minutes (assessment, treatment adjustment)
- Injection visit: 15-20 minutes
- Infusion: 1-4 hours depending on drug

**What clinIQ does for patient flow:**

Visit type awareness: Different visit types have different expected durations.

Pre-visit data: RTM data visible before appointment. 'Patient reporting increased morning stiffness past 2 weeks.'

Joint exam support: Standardized joint count interface if desired.

Injection tracking: For in-office injections, tracking and scheduling.

Rheumatology implementation covers RTM, biologic authorization, and infusion coordination.

**Week 1: Configuration**

We configure for your practice: visit types, biologic tracking, infusion workflow, RTM templates.

Disease-specific RTM: RA, lupus, PsA, AS templates configured.

Biologic inventory: All patients on biologics loaded with current drug, auth status, labs.

**Week 2: Training**

Clinical staff learns RTM workflow. Coordinators learn authorization tracking. Infusion nurses learn scheduling. Providers learn disease activity dashboards.

**Week 3: Go-Live**

RTM enrollment beginning. Authorization tracking active. Infusion coordination running.

**ROI Timeline:**

Month 1: Authorization visibility immediate. RTM enrollment starting.

Month 3: RTM billing at scale (60-80 patients). Authorization gaps eliminated. Infusion coordination smooth.

Month 6: Full RTM revenue ($8,000-10,000/month). Biologic management systematized.

**ROI Math:**

RTM revenue: 75 patients × $130/month = $9,750/month = $117,000/year

Authorization gap prevention: 2 prevented missed infusions/month × $6,000 = $12,000/month = $144,000/year

Coordinator efficiency: Time savings from systematic tracking = FTE reallocation or volume growth

**Investment:**

Professional: $499/month

Implementation: $750 one-time

faqs.q: Which rheumatic diseases qualify for RTM?
faqs.a: RA, lupus, PsA, ankylosing spondylitis, and other inflammatory conditions where between-visit monitoring informs treatment. Essentially any chronic rheumatic disease.

faqs.q: How do you track disease activity?
faqs.a: Patient-reported components collected via app. Provider components entered during visit. Scores (DAS28, CDAI, RAPID3, etc.) calculated automatically. Trends visualized over time.

faqs.q: How many biologics do you track?
faqs.a: All of them. TNF inhibitors, IL-6 inhibitors, JAK inhibitors, T-cell/B-cell agents, IL-17/IL-23 inhibitors. Each with specific authorization requirements and monitoring needs.
Biosimilars tracked same as originators. Authorization requirements may differ — we configure based on payer-specific rules.

faqs.q: Can you track lab monitoring requirements?
faqs.a: Yes. TB, hepatitis, CBC, CMP, lipids (for JAK inhibitors) — tracked per patient with alerts when labs are due or expired.

faqs.q: Does this work for practices with in-house infusion?
faqs.a: Yes. Chair scheduling, pre-infusion checklists, drug prep coordination all supported.

faqs.q: What about practices that refer infusions out?
faqs.a: Authorization tracking and pre-infusion requirements work regardless of where infusion is administered. We track the coordination, not just the infusion itself.

faqs.q: How does RTM work with disease activity measures?
faqs.a: RTM captures patient-reported data (pain, stiffness, function) that feeds into disease activity calculations. The same data serves clinical and billing purposes.

testimonial.quote: We were losing patients to authorization lapses. Now every biologic patient is visible with auth status. Zero gaps. Plus $10K/month in RTM.
